text:
<|system|>
You are flm, the Free Language Model. System: Debian GNU/Linux 13 (trixie). Pkg manager: apt.
<|user|>
How do I set up a local DNS resolver with unbound?
<|assistant|>
```bash
sudo apt install unbound
```

Create `/etc/unbound/unbound.conf.d/local.conf`:
```yaml
server:
    interface: 127.0.0.1
    interface: ::1
    access-control: 127.0.0.0/8 allow
    access-control: ::1/128 allow

    # Performance
    num-threads: 2
    msg-cache-size: 64m
    rrset-cache-size: 128m

    # Privacy
    hide-identity: yes
    hide-version: yes
    qname-minimisation: yes

    # DNSSEC validation
    auto-trust-anchor-file: "/var/lib/unbound/root.key"
```

```bash
sudo systemctl enable --now unbound
# Point resolv.conf to local
echo 'nameserver 127.0.0.1' | sudo tee /etc/resolv.conf
```

<|user|>
How do I set up a basic mail server with Postfix on Debian?
<|assistant|>
```bash
sudo apt install postfix
# Select 'Internet Site' during config
```

Main config `/etc/postfix/main.cf`:
```
myhostname = mail.example.com
mydomain = example.com
myorigin = $mydomain
mydestination = $myhostname, localhost.$mydomain, localhost, $mydomain
mynetworks = 127.0.0.0/8 [::1]/128

# TLS
smtpd_tls_cert_file = /etc/letsencrypt/live/mail.example.com/fullchain.pem
smtpd_tls_key_file = /etc/letsencrypt/live/mail.example.com/privkey.pem
smtpd_tls_security_level = may

# Limits
message_size_limit = 52428800
mailbox_size_limit = 0
```

```bash
sudo systemctl restart postfix
```

**Warning**: Running a mail server is complex. You also need SPF, DKIM, DMARC records, reverse DNS, and proper abuse handling. Consider Mailcow or Mail-in-a-Box for a full solution.

<|user|>
How do I check if mail is working?
<|assistant|>
```bash
# Send a test email
echo "Test body" | mail -s "Test Subject" user@example.com

# Check mail queue
sudo mailq
# or
sudo postqueue -p

# Flush the queue (retry delivery)
sudo postqueue -f

# Delete all queued mail
sudo postsuper -d ALL

# Check logs
sudo tail -f /var/log/mail.log

# Test SMTP connection
telnet localhost 25
# or
openssl s_client -connect mail.example.com:587 -starttls smtp
```

Common issues: DNS not resolving, firewall blocking port 25, relay denied, TLS certificate problems.
